title: Go Formatting (gofmt)
description: Keep your code neat and tidy automatically.

slides.title: The End of Format Wars ⚔️
slides.content:
## Tabs vs Spaces?

For decades, programmers argued over how code should look.
*   "Use 4 spaces for indentation!"
*   "No, use tabs!"
*   "Put the curly brace on a new line!"

Go's creators said: **Enough.**

quizData.question: What common programmer argument did Go attempt to solve?

options.text: How code should visually be formatted

quizData.explanation: Go removes the debate by having one official standard for code formatting.

slides.title: Enter: gofmt 🧹
slides.content:
## The Auto-Formatter

Go comes with a tool named `gofmt` (Go Format).

It takes your messy, jumbled code and automatically reformats it into the **official Go standard**.

Because of `gofmt`, every Go codebase in the world looks exactly the same! This makes reading other people's Go code incredibly easy.

quizData.question: Which tool automatically formats your Go code to the official standard?
quizData.explanation: `gofmt` enforces the community standard styling rules on your code automatically.

slides.title: Strict Semicolons 🚫
slides.content:
## Look Ma, no Semicolons!

Unlike Java, C, or early JavaScript, Go code **does not** require you to put semicolons `;` at the end of every single line.

Behind the scenes, the compiler cleverly inserts them for you. This keeps your screen clean and your eyes happy!

quizData.question: You must put a semicolon `;` at the end of every line in Go.
quizData.explanation: Go does not require semicolons; it inserts them automatically behind the scenes.
